mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in both series fail to produce a positive return probability above 55% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during periods of global supply chain disruptions or commodity price shocks.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation accelerates above 0.3% monthly while the federal funds rate remains below 0.5%, anticipated monetary tightening and margin compression drive negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns exceed 60% of triggered cases despite the inflation-rate divergence over a 4-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth reflects expanding economic output and corporate earnings potential, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP revisions show no statistically significant correlation with subsequent 60-day equity returns with a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm broad-based labor market strength, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 10 basis points per month over a 4-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% indicates a sudden contraction in manufacturing activity and weak downstream demand, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Negative returns occur in less than 45% of triggered instances, or the signal generates a negative Sharpe ratio in walk-forward testing.
